hawkish_phrases: participants generally viewed the risks to the outlook for inflation as having become more balanced than was the case over most of 2020
several participants noted the potential for pandemic-related supply constraints to affect price inflation somewhat more than anticipated
Some participants pointed to the continued increase in market-based measures of inflation compensation
equity valuations had risen further, that initial public offering activity was elevated
risk spreads on corporate bonds and loans were generally low, even with corporate indebtedness having risen to high levels
dovish_phrases: the economy remained far from the Committee's longer-run goals
economic activity and employment were currently well below levels consistent with achieving maximum employment
the pandemic continued to pose considerable risks to the economic outlook
most still viewed the risks as weighted to the downside
it was likely to take some time for substantial further progress to be achieved
summary: The Committee unanimously voted to hold the federal funds rate at 0-0.25% and continue asset purchases at $80 billion Treasuries and $40 billion MBS per month, with a clear easing bias emphasizing the economy remains far from maximum employment and price stability goals amid continued pandemic risks.
